title: Plant Sprout Friend
bio: Peep Rootlet is a plant sprout friend who pops out of teacups and asks the sun polite questions. They feel hopeful, wiggle two leaves, and keep soil crumbs as lucky charms.

steps.title: Base Shapes
steps.instruction: On the Guides layer, draw a small oval head and a short stem body. Add two simple leaf shapes at the top.
steps.hint: Leaves can be tiny teardrops pointing upward.

steps.title: Face Placement
steps.instruction: On the Sketch layer, draw two small eyes on the oval head. Add hopeful raised eyebrows and a tiny round smile.
steps.hint: Place the eyes close together for a sweet baby-sprout look.

steps.title: Personality Details
steps.instruction: On the Sketch layer, add a little soil mound, root feet, cheek dots, leaf veins, and a few crumb shapes around the base.
steps.hint: Root feet can be short curved lines, like tiny toes in the soil.

steps.title: Clean Lineart
steps.instruction: On the Lineart layer, trace the sprout head, stem, leaves, face, soil mound, roots, crumbs, and leaf veins.
steps.hint: Keep leaf veins as one simple line in each leaf.

steps.title: Color & Details
steps.instruction: On the Color + Details layer, fill the sprout, leaves, soil, cheeks, and crumbs. Add a small shadow under the soil mound.
steps.hint: Use a darker green near the stem base for gentle depth.
